# Requirement Quality Checklist: GitHub-style Mentions and References

**Purpose**: Validate that requirements in spec.md are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable (unit tests for requirements writing—not implementation verification).  
**Created**: 2025-03-13  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are detection and styling requirements defined for all four pattern types (@username, @org/team, #123, @user/repo#456)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001–FR-004]
- [x] CHK002 Is the “GitHub context” (when links are clickable) defined with both auto-detect and override behavior? [Completeness, Spec §Context, Assumptions]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements specified for both “in context” (clickable) and “not in context” (styled only, no errors) behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are accessibility requirements specified for mention/issue reference (link semantics when clickable, styled text when not)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK005 Is reveal-on-select behavior specified for both “select” and “deselect” states? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “username” for @username defined with explicit character rules (alphanumeric and hyphen, no leading hyphen)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK007 Is “valid email” for exclusion defined or scoped (e.g. pattern that looks like email) so implementers can avoid false positives? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK008 Is “same interaction used for other links in the editor” specified or referenced so link handling is unambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK009 Is “styled distinctly” or “visually distinct” defined or tied to measurable success criteria (e.g. SC-001)? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, SC-001]
- [x] CHK010 Are “segment after @” and “one slash” for @org/team defined precisely enough to avoid overlap with @user/repo#456? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, FR-004]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do pattern requirements (FR-001–FR-004) align with edge-case exclusions (email, code blocks) and with contract/pattern docs? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001–FR-007, contracts/]
- [x] CHK012 Are link-handling and accessibility requirements consistent (clickable ⇒ link semantics; not clickable ⇒ styled text only)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, FR-011]
- [x] CHK013 Do Key Entities (Mention, Issue/Reference, Context) align with functional requirements and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Is “under one second” for SC-001 specified in a way that can be verified (e.g. open/switch document, then check)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK015 Are “zero false positives” (SC-003) and “representative test data” scoped so acceptance is testable? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK016 Can “no overlap or missed tokens” (SC-005) be objectively verified from the requirement text? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK017 Are success criteria technology-agnostic (no implementation details)? [Acceptance Criteria Quality, Spec §SC-001–SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary flows (styled display, click in context, reveal on select) covered by both requirements and user stories? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1–3, FR-001–FR-011]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate flows (e.g. user disables context via setting) addressed in requirements or assumptions? [Coverage, Spec §Context, Assumptions]
- [x] CHK020 Are exception flows (malformed input, no git repo, missing remote) addressed so “MUST NOT break” is testable? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-006]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Is the boundary between mention and email explicitly defined (e.g. “looks like email” or pattern rule)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-007, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are “Issue #123” / “see #456” (preceding text) explicitly in scope with only #digits styled? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK023 Is behavior when multiple mentions/refs are adjacent or overlapping addressed in requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-010, SC-005]
- [x] CHK024 Are code block / inline code exclusions for mention/ref detection specified in spec or referenced contracts? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-001–FR-004, contracts/mention-issue-patterns.md]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK025 Are performance expectations for decoration/parse (e.g. “under one second”) stated in success criteria or plan? [NFR, Spec §SC-001, plan.md]
- [x] CHK026 Are accessibility requirements for assistive technology (link vs text exposure) specified and consistent? [NFR, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK027 Is “MUST NOT break or show errors” when not in context specified as a non-functional constraint? [NFR, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Is the dependency on “existing markdown link behavior” documented and valid for the extension? [Dependency, Spec §FR-006, Assumptions]
- [x] CHK029 Are assumptions about “GitHub Flavored Markdown conventions” and “reveal raw” interaction model documented? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK030 Is the optional user/workspace override for “GitHub context” clearly documented as an assumption? [Assumption, Spec §Context, Clarifications]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 Is “optional target URL when in a resolving context” (Key Entities) aligned with “URL computed at link-provider time” in plan/research? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, plan/research]
- [x] CHK032 Are there any conflicting definitions of “in context” (spec vs plan vs contracts)? [Conflict, Spec §FR-006, contracts/forge-context-api.md]
- [x] CHK033 Is “representative samples” / “representative test data” (SC-003, SC-005) defined or left intentionally flexible? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-003, SC-005]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Each item tests requirement quality (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, coverage)—not implementation behavior.
- Add comments or findings inline; link to spec section or contract when resolving gaps.
